# terrible_theforeman_foreman_content_view_filter_rule (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `content_view` (String) the name of the content view that the filter applies to
- `content_view_filter` (String) the name of the content view filter that the rule applies to
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `architecture` (String) set package, module_stream, etc. architecture that the rule applies to
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `context` (String) the context for a module only valid in filter *type=modulemd*
- `date_type` (String) set whether rule applied to erratum using the 'Issued On' or 'Updated On' date only valid on filter *type=erratum*.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `end_date` (String) the rule limit for erratum end date (YYYY-MM-DD) see date_type for the date the rule applies to Only valid on *filter_type=erratum_by_date*.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `errata_id` (String) erratum id
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `max_version` (String) package maximum version
- `min_version` (String) package minimum version
- `name` (String) Content view filter rule name, package name, package_group name, module stream or docker tag If omitted, the value of *name* will be used if necessary for module stream filters, this is the name of the module stream to search for
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `start_date` (String) the rule limit for erratum start date (YYYY-MM-DD) see date_type for the date the rule applies to Only valid on *filter_type=erratum*.
- `stream` (String) the context for a module only valid in filter *type=modulemd*
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `types` (String) errata types the ruel applies to (enhancement, bugfix, security) Only valid on *filter_type=erratum*
- `version` (String) package or module version

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `entity` (String) Final state of the affected entities grouped by their type.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
